name: ResumeGraph: Autonomous Multi-Model Resume Generation Pipeline
raw_facts: Architecture: Developed a stateful, multi-node AI orchestration pipeline using LangGraph to automate resume customization based on Job Descriptions (JDs).
Orchestration: Engineered a 3-stage graph-based workflow (JD Extraction -> Vector Retrieval -> ATS-Optimized Rewriting) with deterministic state transfers.
Reliability: Built a `KeyManager` system for automated round-robin rotation of 4+ Google API keys to mitigate `429 Resource Exhausted` errors and ensure high availability.
Data Retrieval: Integrated Qdrant hybrid search to perform context-aware retrieval of top experience and project bullets, providing factual grounding for LLM generation.
Batch Processing: Scaled the pipeline to handle concurrent JD processing from a local directory (`jd_inputs/`), automating PDF generation for multiple roles in a single execution.
Algorithms: Implemented a robust chronological sorting engine using semi-structured date parsing ('Jan 2021 - Present') to ensure professional experience is perfectly ordered.
Technical Synthesis: Enforced a strict 'Problem-Action-Result' (PAR) narrative pattern for all generated bullet points to maximize professional impact.
Output Engineering: Comiled dynamic LaTeX templates via Jinja2 and MiKTeX, with automated cleanup of intermediate `.tex`, `.log`, and `.aux` files.
business_impact: Reduced resume customization time from hours to seconds per application, while maintaining 100% factual accuracy through vector-grounded retrieval.
Enabled large-scale job search automation by supporting batch processing and robust API rate-limit handling.
